notes: [Verified 2026-03-17 | Surg Endosc 2006 | content_match] Morgenstern L. 'George Berci: past, present, and future.' Surg Endosc. 2006 May;20 Suppl 2:S493-4. Written by Leon Morgenstern, the Chair of Surgery at Cedars-Sinai who recruited Berci in 1967. Documents Berci's 'contributions to the art and science of endoscopy and laparoscopy' over 'nearly half a century.' Berci (1921-2024) was born in Szeged, Hungary, graduated University of Szeged 1950, fled during Hungarian Revolution 1956 on a Rockefeller Fellowship to Melbourne. In Australia he developed the first miniature endoscopic camera (1962). Recruited to Cedars-Sinai 1967 as visiting scholar, became director of the multidisciplinary surgical endoscopy unit ~1970, and remained active until his death at age 103. The program he founded was the first multidisciplinary surgical endoscopy division in the US. Berci goes from degree 1 (SAGES founder) to degree 2. This creates a West Coast institutional anchor for the MIS module — previously all US MIS nodes were Midwest/South (Ponsky/Cleveland, Reddick/Nashville).

notes: Ponsky served as a founding force, ensuring surgical endoscopy remained a core competency.

Marescaux founded IRCAD as a dedicated research and training institute for minimally invasive and robotic surgery.
